items.title: Geospatial hotspot and event detection
items.description: Cluster GPS or check-in coordinates into dense activity regions (venues, protests, traffic jams) while marking sparse transit paths as noise — no need to preset the number of hotspots.

items.title: Anomaly and outlier flagging
items.description: Points that fail to join any density-connected cluster receive noise labels — useful for fraud, intrusion, or sensor fault screening alongside normal operating modes.

items.title: Image segmentation preprocessing
items.description: Color or texture features in pixel neighborhoods can be clustered into regions with arbitrary boundaries; DBSCAN handles non-convex shapes better than centroid-based methods.

items.title: Customer micro-segment discovery
items.description: Behavioral feature vectors reveal natural dense segments (power users, dormant accounts) without forcing k equal market slices — sparse accounts remain noise for separate campaigns.

items.title: Robotics and LiDAR point grouping
items.description: 3D point clouds form objects as dense patches separated by empty space; density clustering separates obstacles from background without specifying object count upfront.
